text:
COMBINED RESTAGING EXAMINATION
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with stage IV lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, with acquired T790M resistance on osimertinib. Previously completed Gamma Knife stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to three intracranial metastases (right cerebellum, left frontal, right parietal) approximately 10 weeks ago. Currently on carboplatin/pemetrexed, now after cycle 3 (carboplatin dose-reduced to AUC 4 following grade 3 thrombocytopenia in cycle 2; pemetrexed maintained at 500 mg/m2). Restaging to assess systemic and intracranial treatment response.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with intravenous iohexol contrast in the portal venous phase following standard premedication protocol. Coronal and sagittal reformats generated and reviewed. Comparison: Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained approximately 10 weeks ago at time of treatment initiation.

FINDINGS:

Chest:

Lungs and Airways: The right lower lobe mass has decreased in size compared to the prior examination. The mass now measures approximately 2.4 x 2.1 cm in the axial plane (previously 3.1 x 2.8 cm), representing meaningful interval shrinkage. The lesion maintains its irregular, spiculated morphology with adjacent pleural tethering. The surrounding parenchyma demonstrates some residual peritumoral consolidation that appears slightly improved. Airway patency is maintained throughout the central and segmental bronchi. No endobronchial lesions. No new pulmonary nodules are identified on this examination. The previously noted bilateral small pulmonary nodules (largest 8 mm right upper lobe on prior prior staging CT) are no longer discretely visualized and appear resolved. Lung windows demonstrate no ground-glass opacities, no interstitial thickening, and no evidence of treatment-related pneumonitis at this time.

Mediastinum and Hila: The previously enlarged right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node, which had re-enlarged to 1.2 cm at the time of progression, now measures approximately 0.9 cm in short axis, slightly reduced. No discrete station 7 (subcarinal) adenopathy. No bulky mediastinal disease. Heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.

Pleura: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No rib destruction. No soft tissue chest wall involvement.

Abdomen:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No focal hepatic lesions. No intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. No hepatic metastases.

Adrenal Glands: The right adrenal gland contains an ovoid, mildly heterogeneous soft tissue nodule measuring approximately 1.4 x 1.2 cm (previously 1.4 x 1.3 cm), essentially unchanged in size. Enhancement pattern remains consistent with prior examination. No new adrenal lesions on the left. The stability of this lesion over the course of 10 weeks is noted.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically without evidence of hydronephrosis or mass lesions. No ureteral obstruction.

Pancreas: Normal morphology and attenuation. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fluid.

Spleen: Normal size and homogeneous enhancement. No splenic lesions.

Bowel and Mesentery: No bowel obstruction. No mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy of pathologic size. No free intraabdominal fluid.

Vasculature: The aorta and its major abdominal branches are patent. No significant atherosclerotic disease.

Pelvis:

No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Uterus and ovaries are atrophic in appearance, appropriate for patient age. No pelvic free fluid. Bladder appears unremarkable.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen/Pelvis): No suspicious osseous lesions in the imaged portions of the lumbar spine, sacrum, or pelvic bones.

IMPRESSION (CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS):
1. The right lower lobe primary mass has decreased in size, now measuring approximately 2.4 cm compared to 3.1 cm on prior examination, consistent with a meaningful response to carboplatin/pemetrexed chemotherapy.
2. The previously noted bilateral small pulmonary nodules are no longer visualized, representing interval resolution.
3. The right adrenal nodule is essentially unchanged at 1.4 cm, appearing stable compared to prior imaging.
4. Slight reduction in size of previously noted right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node, now subcentimeter. No new mediastinal adenopathy.
5. No new sites of thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic metastatic disease identified.
6. No treatment-related pneumonitis.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain was performed at 3.0 Tesla with pre- and post-gadolinium sequences including T1-weighted, T2-weighted FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI), and post-contrast T1 with volumetric acquisition. Comparison: Prior brain MRI obtained approximately 10 weeks ago immediately prior to Gamma Knife SRS, and SRS planning images reviewed for lesion localization.

FINDINGS:

Treated Intracranial Lesions:

Right Cerebellar Hemisphere (SRS target 1): The previously identified 1.1 cm enhancing metastasis treated with 20 Gy in 1 fraction now demonstrates a markedly reduced, centrally necrotic focus. Post-contrast images show a ring of peripheral enhancement measuring approximately 0.6 cm overall, with loss of central enhancement and a hypointense central core on T1 pre-contrast. The surrounding vasogenic edema has substantially improved compared to pre-SRS imaging. The changes are most consistent with a favorable post-treatment response with central necrosis. No evidence of new enhancement in the adjacent cerebellar parenchyma.

Left Frontal Lobe (SRS target 2): The previously identified 0.8 cm enhancing lesion treated with 22 Gy in 1 fraction now measures approximately 0.4 cm on post-contrast sequences. There is diminished but still present peripheral enhancement. The surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality has decreased. The overall appearance reflects a positive therapeutic response. No new left frontal lobe lesions.

Right Parietal Lobe (SRS target 3): The previously identified 0.6 cm enhancing lesion treated with 24 Gy in 1 fraction now measures approximately 0.3 cm on post-contrast images, with only faint residual enhancement. Minimal surrounding T2 signal change. No expansion or new enhancement in the adjacent parietal cortex.

Remainder of Brain Parenchyma: No new enhancing lesions identified throughout the cerebral hemispheres, cerebellum, or brainstem. No new areas of diffusion restriction to suggest acute ischemia. No hemorrhage on SWI sequences. Ventricles and sulci are normal in caliber for patient age. No midline shift. No hydrocephalus.

Leptomeninges: No leptomeningeal enhancement. No superficial cortical spreading enhancement suspicious for leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.

Extra-axial Spaces: No subdural or epidural collections. No extra-axial lesions.

Vasculature: Major intracranial vessels appear patent on routine sequences. No flow void abnormalities.

Sella/Skull Base/Calvarium: No osseous destructive lesions. Pituitary gland appears normal.

IMPRESSION (MRI BRAIN):
1. All three previously treated intracranial metastases (right cerebellum, left frontal lobe, right parietal lobe) demonstrate interval reduction in size and enhancement, with the right cerebellar lesion showing central necrosis, consistent with a favorable response to Gamma Knife stereotactic radiosurgery.
2. No new intracranial metastatic lesions identified.
3. No leptomeningeal disease.
4. The post-treatment changes in the cerebellum are evolving in the expected fashion; continued imaging surveillance recommended to monitor for any late radiation effects.

---

OVERALL IMPRESSION:
Restaging imaging after 3 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed chemotherapy demonstrates a favorable response in the chest, with shrinkage of the right lower lobe primary mass and resolution of previously noted bilateral small pulmonary nodules. The right adrenal metastasis remains essentially stable. No new sites of systemic metastatic disease. Intracranially, all three SRS-treated brain metastases show continued reduction in size and enhancement consistent with an excellent treatment response. No new brain lesions or leptomeningeal disease.
